slide_003.appears_in: In Java, such a group of objects manipulated as a single entity is called a collection ◮ Java language environment (JDK) provides a Collections Framework that hold together several classes and interfaces of the type collection ◮ Quiz: are you able to differentiate between Java, JVM and JDK?

slide_004.appears_in: Collections vs ADTs ► There are familiar composite data structures behaving as collections called Abstract Data Types (ADT) Main among these are the List, Stack (LIFO), Queue (FIFO), Tree, and Table ►

Collections vs ADTs ► There are familiar composite data structures behaving as collections called Abstract Data Types (ADT) Main among these are the List, Stack (LIFO), Queue (FIFO), Tree, and Table ►

Collections vs ADTs ► There are familiar composite data structures behaving as collections called Abstract Data Types (ADT) Main among these are the List, Stack (LIFO), Queue (FIFO), Tree, and Table ►

Each of these ADTs have unique interfaces (methods) that clearly identify the specific ADT through its behaviour While ADTs are useful, not having a common interface for manipulating these collections was a disadvantage ▶

slide_005.appears_in: In the Java Collections Framework, there are 2 main root interface classes: 1.

Java pre- and post- Collections ◮ Object-oriented program designs can benefit from a framework of classes providing the functionality of a collection if they all perform similar tasks in a similar manner ◮ Originally, JDK provided the ability to group objects through Arrays (as a native language feature) and Vectors or Hashtables (as utility classes) ◮

slide_008.appears_in: Advantages of the Collection Framework ◮ Consistent API: The API has a basic set of interfaces (Collection, Set, List, Queue, Deque, Map, etc) and a set of classes that implement these interfaces (HashSet, TreeSet, ArrayList, LinkedList, ArrayDeque, HashMap, TreeMap, Vector, etc) ◮ All the implementation classes have some common set of methods ◮ Reduced programming effort: Due to the use of a common set of conceptually abstract methods, programmers can effectively use the OOP concepts of Abstraction and Encapsulation ◮ Increased program performance: Availability of high-performance implementations of essential data structures and algorithms improves both memory use and run-time efficiency

Advantages of the Collection Framework ◮ Consistent API: The API has a basic set of interfaces (Collection, Set, List, Queue, Deque, Map, etc) and a set of classes that implement these interfaces (HashSet, TreeSet, ArrayList, LinkedList, ArrayDeque, HashMap, TreeMap, Vector, etc) ◮ All the implementation classes have some common set of methods ◮ Reduced programming effort: Due to the use of a common set of conceptually abstract methods, programmers can effectively use the OOP concepts of Abstraction and Encapsulation ◮ Increased program performance: Availability of high-performance implementations of essential data structures and algorithms improves both memory use and run-time efficiency

slide_010.appears_in: HashMap Class ◮ HashMap<Key, Value> class is an implementation of the Java Map interface and stores data as (Key, Value) pairs ◮ The Key and Value can be different types of objects and the Key is the index for accessing a Value from the collection ◮

slide_015.appears_in: LinkedList Class ◮ LinkedList class is an implementation of the linked list data structure where elements are linked using pointers and addresses ◮ LinkedList behaviour is that of a dynamic array that grows (or contracts) in size as items are added or removed ◮
The linked list is implemented as a doubly linked list data structure allowing for efficient insertion and removal of items from the list ◮

LinkedList Class ◮ LinkedList class is an implementation of the linked list data structure where elements are linked using pointers and addresses ◮ LinkedList behaviour is that of a dynamic array that grows (or contracts) in size as items are added or removed ◮
Default constructor LinkedList() creates an empty linked list 2.
Constructor LinkedList(Collection col) creates an instance that contains all the elements of a specified collection col.

ArrayList Class ◮ ArrayList class is an implementation of a dynamic array data structure where the size of the array automatically changes when items are dynamically added (or removed) ◮

slide_019.appears_in: ArrayList Implementation 1/2 ◮ There are 3 methods to create an instance of a ArrayList object 1.
Default constructor ArrayList() creates an empty array list 2.
Constructor ArrayList(Collection col) creates an instance that contains all the elements of a specified collection col.

Constructor ArrayList(int capacity) creates an instance with the specified initial capacity ◮ JVM implements ArrayList as an array of objects with each objects ◮ ArrayList object overhead = 32 bytes ◮ Default capacity of a ArrayList object is 10 entries ◮ Size of an empty ArrayList using default parameters is 88 bytes ◮ ArrayList\$Entry object overhead = 24 bytes
